outcome_text: Practices and personnel for supporting regular engagement with relevant AI actors and integrating feedback about positive, negative, and unanticipated impacts are in place and documented.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI stakeholder engagement practices, including establishing regular engagement mechanisms with affected communities, designing feedback integration processes for unanticipated impacts, and documenting engagement personnel and practices for ongoing AI impact monitoring.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI stakeholder engagement practices, including establishing regular engagement mechanisms with affected communities, designing feedback integration processes for unanticipated impacts, and documenting engagement personnel and practices for ongoing AI impact monitoring.
reference_document_element_description: Manage the data and AI adoption program, including stakeholder engagement plans, adoption milestone tracking, barrier identification, and intervention deployment.
comments: NIST AI RMF MP-5.2 (MAP) superset of WIDAI OP domain. OP-T-005 is the strongest match (STS: 0.625, cosine: 0.675). Secondary: AG-S-004 (AG, cosine: 0.656), AG-K-006 (AG, cosine: 0.652).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.2/10 (raw 0.625). Shared vocabulary: [ai, engagement, stakeholder]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [affected, communities, designing, documenting, establishing, feedback] vs WIDAI terms [adoption, barrier, data, deployment, identification, intervention] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.888 (P=0.880, R=0.896). Balanced P/R — the 32-word competency implication and 19-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (98.6%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.675 > STS 0.625 (gap +0.050). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, engagement, stakeholder] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 13.2%. Shared tokens: [ai, engagement, stakeholder]. NIST AI RMF-only: [affected, communities, designing, documenting, establishing, feedback]. WIDAI-only: [adoption, barrier, data, deployment, identification, intervention].
